title: Bad associative array default needs a verilator error instead of a c++ compile error
body:
Here's a simple example of something I ran into:
```verilog
module top;
    typedef byte unsigned word_t[4];
    word_t words[longint unsigned] = '{default:'1};

    initial begin
        for(int i = 0; i < 4; i++) begin
            $display("words[23][%0d]: 'h%h", i, words[23][i]);
        end
    end
endmodule
```
With a commercial simulator it just silently converts that `1 to a value that "works" (which is not very helpful), with verilator I get a C++ compiler error (which is hard to decipher):
```
In file included from Vassociative_array_of_array_with_default__ALL.cpp:8:
Vassociative_array_of_array_with_default___024root__0__Slow.cpp: In function ‘void Vassociative_array_of_array_with_default___024root___eval_initial__TOP(Vassociative_array_of_array_with_default___024root*)’:
Vassociative_array_of_array_with_default___024root__0__Slow.cpp:31:30: error: cannot convert ‘unsigned int’ to ‘const VlUnpacked<unsigned char, 4>&’
   31 |                  .setDefault(0xffU).at(0x0000000000000017ULL)
      |                              ^~~~~
      |                              |
      |                              unsigned int
```
Seems like it should be caught during verilation.

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

verilator -j "$(nproc)" --binary <filename>

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?

Latest master: g996ae35a1

What OS and distribution are you using?

rocky linux 9

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

sure
